title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During a visit to the Erskine residence, Gwendolyn and Jiles meet with Colonel and Mrs. Erskine. Eustace Erskine discusses his two sons—one in the military and one recently graduated from Oxford—and expresses pride in the sacrifices he made for them. Gwendolyn observes a sense of darkness in the atmosphere and notes Mrs. Erskine's apparent jealousy. When Jiles mentions they are looking for a house in Deal, Mrs. Erskine reacts coldly, stating they lived there years ago and disliked the "lazy life." A moment of tension occurs when Jiles identifies a nearby house as St. Catherine's Cottage rather than the name Mrs. Erskine suggests, causing a visible reaction from the couple. Mrs. Erskine then excuses herself to the garden, where the Colonel tends to flowers.

After leaving, Gwendolyn and Jiles discuss their mission. Gwendolyn reveals she left a ring near some forget-me-nots to facilitate a pretext for returning. Jiles expresses guilt over Gwendolyn performing the "dirty work" of investigating, while Gwendolyn argues that a woman can ask personal questions more easily than a man. She expresses doubt that Colonel Erskine is the killer, believing he is a good man who loved Helen.

The following morning, Jiles monitors Anstey Manor while Gwendolyn returns to the Erskine garden under the pretext of searching for her lost engagement ring. After finding the ring near some larkspurs, Gwendolyn sits with Colonel Erskine and confronts him about his past relationship with her stepmother, Helen Kennedy (later Helen Harley). Erskine remains composed but tense, acknowledging that Gwendolyn is likely seeking information regarding certain letters.
